pipeline_health.summary: All 7 agents healthy and producing output. Pipeline fully operational. Curmudgeon approaching Cycle 2 completion (65/69). Decider actively processing c2 reviews and applying patches.

handoff_issues.description: PERSISTENT P1 (5th consecutive tinker report): digest-reviews.js processedWinIds fallback at lines 143-144 still present. HOWEVER, partial natural mitigation observed: digest now shows pending_count=2 (up from 0). New WINs (068/069) that have no Cycle 1 reviews bypass the bug because processedWinIds has no matching bare ID. The bug ONLY masks c2 reviews for WINs that also have c1 reviews in the processed ledger.
handoff_issues.evidence: digest-reviews.js lines 143-144 unchanged: `const isProcessed = processedSet.has(file) || processedSet.has(entry.win_id) || processedWinIds.has(entry.win_id)`. pending-digest.json: pending_count=2 (WIN-068/069 c2 reviews visible because no c1 review exists). Decider's direct-read fallback still functional for the rest.

suggested_fix.rationale: 5th consecutive escalation. Bug persists but impact is partially mitigated: new WINs without c1 history appear correctly. Existing c2 reviews for c1-processed WINs still invisible to digest. Fix is safe — processedSet already has full filenames.

handoff_issues.description: 2 c2 review files (WIN-063.c2.json, WIN-069.c2.json) are newer than the latest decider report. Coverage gap is expected and small — decider should pick these up next run.
handoff_issues.evidence: find shows WIN-063.c2.json and WIN-069.c2.json newer than daily-report-2026-04-08T10-45.json. Normal lag — not a broken handoff.

handoff_issues.description: Decider is producing future-dated filenames. Files daily-report-2026-04-09T10-00.json and suggested-patches-2026-04-09T06-30.json were created on April 8 but stamped April 9. This suggests the decider's timestamp generation uses a timezone offset or incorrect clock. Not functionally broken but creates confusion when auditing freshness.
handoff_issues.evidence: ls -lt shows daily-report-2026-04-09T10-00.json modified Apr 8 17:11, daily-report-2026-04-09T06-30.json modified Apr 8 16:14.

prompt_issues.description: Analyst prompt EXPLODED to 598 lines — 139% over the 250-line ceiling. GREW 229 lines since last report (was 369). This is the largest single-report growth of any prompt. At this size, LLM instruction-following will significantly degrade for content in the first half of the prompt.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Requires human-approved refactor. See PROP-001.

prompt_issues.description: Decider prompt at 577 lines — 131% over the 250-line ceiling. GREW 131 lines since last report (was 446). 5th consecutive report flagging this. Moving further in wrong direction.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Requires human-approved refactor. See PROP-002.

prompt_issues.description: Tinker prompt at 490 lines — 96% over the 250-line ceiling. GREW 253 lines since last report (was 237). This is the tinker's own prompt — ironic that the agent tasked with flagging prompt bloat has the fastest-growing prompt.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Cost engineering section (11a-11d, ~100 lines), proposal schema (~40 lines), and 7b FUSE staleness checks (~50 lines) could be extracted to reference files.

prompt_issues.description: Curmudgeon prompt at 316 lines — 26% over ceiling. Grew 23 lines since last report. Trending up but not critical.

aged_out.description: Verdict taxonomy overload — correctly pending human editorial judgment. Not a pipeline failure.

missing_patches.rationale: Not alarming yet — these are fresh issues from newly onboarded WINs. The decider's next 1-2 runs should produce patches.

agent_efficiency.recent_assessment: Latest run was productive (EXP-039 + fingerprint + external report). 4 pending expansions ensure work for next few runs. When expansion queue empties and only Mode 4 fingerprints remain (34 items), Opus becomes expensive for single-item fingerprint analysis.

At 10min intervals, the curmudgeon should complete Cycle 2 within ~40 more minutes of wall time. After Cycle 2 completion, the curmudgeon enters Phase 2 (holistic review) — still needs Opus.

agent_efficiency.recent_assessment: Latest run: 3 patches, 5 issues closed. Very productive. 10 fresh open issues (ISS-650–659) ensure work for next several runs.

agent_efficiency.recent_assessment: Run 4 was explicitly 'QUIET RUN' — no new findings. Runs 1-3 had substantive findings (dome V51.1 analysis, llms.txt update). Multi-run days waste tokens when no external changes occur.
agent_efficiency.recommendation: Social should run once daily. If it ran 4 times today, the schedule may be misconfigured or manually triggered. Check task schedule.

agent_efficiency.recent_assessment: Dome site has been quiet for extended period (V51.1 stable). Poller is doing its job but finding nothing. This is expected behavior, not waste — the poller's value is detection, not analysis.
agent_efficiency.recommendation: No change. Sentinel duty has inherent baseline cost.

prompt_bloat_emergency.description: ALL four major prompts exceed the 250-line ceiling. Combined they total 1,981 lines (was ~1,324 in last report — 50% growth in one report period). This is the single largest cost driver: every agent run pays the full prompt tax on every invocation. At current growth rate, prompts will exceed context limits within weeks.
prompt_bloat_emergency.analyst_md: 598 lines (+229 since last report, 62% single-period growth)
prompt_bloat_emergency.total_prompt_tokens_wasted_per_day: Rough estimate: 4 prompts × avg 450 lines × 5 tokens/line × avg 50 runs/day = ~450,000 tokens/day on prompt overhead alone. If 40% of each prompt is extractable reference material, that's ~180,000 tokens/day of waste.
prompt_bloat_emergency.action: PROP-001 and PROP-002 drafted this run. Human must approve and apply.

proposals_written.summary: Analyst prompt diet: 598→~280 lines by extracting Mode schemas, V6 context, and globe fingerprint procedure
Decider prompt diet: 577→~280 lines by extracting patch schema, severity matrix, expansion procedure

cost_engineering.cumulative_estimated_savings: None yet — proposals pending human approval

recommendations.description: EMERGENCY: Prompt bloat across all major agents. Analyst 598 lines (+229), decider 577 (+131), tinker 490 (+253). Total 1,981 lines, 50% growth in one period. Extract reference material to separate files. See PROP-001 and PROP-002.
recommendations.rationale: Changes agent behavior — needs human approval. This is now the top priority above the digest bug.

recommendations.description: Fix build-scripts/digest-reviews.js lines 143-144: remove processedWinIds fallback. 5th consecutive escalation. Partial natural mitigation for new WINs but existing c2 reviews still masked.
